questions.text: What does 'Ad-Duha' refer to?
questions.answer: The morning brightness or the forenoon.

questions.text: What is the second oath in the Surah?
questions.answer: The night when it covers with darkness/is still.

questions.text: What was the rumor the disbelievers spread that this Surah refutes?
questions.answer: They said that Allah had forsaken Prophet Muhammad (SAW) because revelation had paused.

questions.text: What is the reassurance given in Verse 3?
questions.answer: Your Lord has not taken leave of you, nor has He detested you.

questions.text: How does the Hereafter compare to the first [life] for the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: The Hereafter is better for him than the first (worldly) life.

questions.text: What is the promise in Verse 5?
questions.answer: Your Lord is going to give to you, and you will be satisfied.

questions.text: What three states of the Prophet's early life are mentioned in Verses 6-8?
questions.answer: He was an orphan, he was wandering/lost (seeking truth), and he was poor/in need.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state as an orphan?
questions.answer: He found him an orphan and gave him shelter.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state of 'wandering'?
questions.answer: He found him lost/wandering and guided him.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state of 'need'?
questions.answer: He found him in need and made him self-sufficient/wealthy.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the orphan (Verse 9)?
questions.answer: Do not oppress or treat the orphan with harshness.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the petitioner/beggar (Verse 10)?
questions.answer: Do not repel or scold the one who asks.

questions.text: What should the Prophet (and believers) do regarding the favor of their Lord?
questions.answer: Proclaim it / Speak of it (Verse 11).

questions.text: What is the 'favor' (Ni'mah) referred to at the end?
questions.answer: It includes the Quran, Prophethood, and all spiritual and material blessings.

questions.text: What is the emotional tone of Surah Ad-Duha?
questions.answer: One of intense comfort, affection, and reassurance.

questions.text: True or False: This Surah teaches that past help from Allah is a proof of future support.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: Why is 'the night when it is still' mentioned after 'the morning brightness'?
questions.answer: It may represent the pause in revelation, which is as natural and necessary as the night following the day.

questions.text: What is the relationship between Verse 5 and the concept of 'Shafa'ah' (Intercession)?
questions.answer: Scholars say the Prophet (SAW) will not be satisfied until his entire Ummah is forgiven.

questions.text: What does 'Fa-Aghna' (made self-sufficient) mean for the Prophet?
questions.answer: Contentment of the heart and the wealth Khadijah (RA) brought to him.

questions.text: How does this Surah instruct us to handle our past?
questions.answer: By reflecting on how Allah took care of us when we were vulnerable, to strengthen our current faith.
